THIS CASE COMES ON CALENDAR, HAVING BEEN SET AT THE REQUEST OF THE PLAINTIFF, FOR A SECOND ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: CONTEMPT OF COURT AND FURTHER ORDERS ENFORCING INJUNCTION. THE COURT FINDS THE DEFENDANT JAMES CHAU IN CONTEMPT OF COURT FOR VIOLATING THIS COURT'S JUNE 4, 2008 ORDER BY FAILING, WITHOUT GOOD CAUSE, TO PAY PLAINTIFF'S $20,974 WITHIN 30 DAYS OF THE ENTRY OF THAT ORDER, AS ORDERED. SEE SIGNED/SCANNED ORDER.JUDGE SUZANNE BOLANOS, CSR: NICI RODICH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA (602).
CAUSE IS CONTINUED TO NOV-19-2008 AT 10:00 AM IN 602 FOR EXAMINATION OF JUDGMENT DEBTOR JAMES CHAU. JUDGE SUZANNE BOLANOS, CSR: NICI RODICH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA (602).

STIPULATED PERMANENT INJUNCTION

STIPULATED PERMANENT INJUNCTION APPROVED AND SIGNED BY BOTH PARTIES AND THE COURT AND FILED ON THIS DATE. INJUNCTION WILL TAKE EFFECT ON FEBRUARY 15, 2008. PARTIES SUBMIT AND FILE STIPULATED STATEMENT OF UNCONTESTED FACTS, EVIDENCE AND LAW OF LIEU OF TRIAL IN LIEU OF PRESENTING EVIDENCE AND TESTIMONY AT TRIAL. THIS MATTER HAS SETTLED WITH EXCEPTION TO THE ISSUE OF DOLLAR AMOUNT OF FINE TO BE IMPOSED. THIS REMAINING MATTER TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION ON FEB-15-2008 BY SUZANNE R. BOLANOS. JUDGE SUZANNE BOLANOS, CSR: PEPPINA THOMPSON, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA (602).

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS HEREBY ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED: 1) PURSUANT TO SF BUILDING CODE SECTION 103, SF HOUSING CODE SECTION 204(D)(2), CA BUSINESS AND PROFESSIONS CODE SECTION 17200 ET SEQ., AND CALIFORNIA HEALTH AND SAFETY CODE SECTION 17980.7(D)(1), DEFENDANT JAMES H.V. CHAU PAY PLAINTIFFS CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO AND PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA CIVIL PENALTIES, FEES AND COSTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $400,000. 2) PAYMENT OF THE $400,000 MAY BE PAID IN TWO PAYMENTS. THE FIRST PAYMENT MUST BE AT LEAST 50% OF THE TOTAL AWARD, AND MUST BE PAID WITHIN 5 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS JUDGMENT. THE BALANCE MUST BE PAID IN FULL WITHIN 120 DAYS OF ENTRY OF THIS JUDGMENT. 3) PAYMENT SHALL BE MADE BY CASHIER'S CHECK OR MONEY ORDER, MADE PAYABLE TO THE CITY ATTORNEY'S OFFICE, 1390 MARKET STREET, 6TH FLOOR, SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94102. ATTN: MICHAEL S. WEISS, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY. 4) IF DEFENDANT FAILS TO MAKE PAYMENT IN THE MANNER AND/OR IN THE TIME FRAME PROVIDED HEREIN, PLAINTIFFS MAY RETURN TO THIS COURT FOR FURTHER COURT ORDERS, INCLUDING AN ORDER IMPOSING 10% INTEREST ON THE ENTIRE AMOUNT OWING, DATING BACK TO THE DATE OF THE ENTRY OF THIS JUDGMENT. 5) THIS JUDGMENT SHALL BE RECORDED AGAINST THE TITLE TO THE SUBJECT PROPERTY. UPON RECEIPT OF THE FULL PAYMENT OF ALL THE PENALTIES DESCRIBED HEREIN, TOGETHER WITH ANY INTEREST OR ADDITIONAL FEES THEREON, PLAINTIFFS WILL, UPON REQUEST, FILE AND RECORD AND ACKNOWLEDGEMENT OF SATISFACTION OF JUDGMENT WITH RESPECT TO THIS JUDGMENT, LEAVING THE STIPULATED INJUNCTION IN FULL FORCE AND EFFECT FOR ITS DURATION. 6) PLAINTIFFS SHALL BE ENTITLED TO ALL REASONABLE ATTORNEYS' FEES AND COSTS INCURRED IN THE COLLECTION OF ANY PAYMENT DESCRIBED HEREIN. SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Discovery Responses From Deft To Impose Monetary Sanctions Against Deft James H Chau
Off Calendar Jun-06-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff City and County of San Francisco's motion to compel responses to form and special interrogatories and request for production of documents is granted. The Court has read and considered the late-filed opposition. Defendant is ordered to serve complete verified responses without objections within 15 days of notice of this order. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $500 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.
